what's inode data and non-journaled quota you mean?

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

Hi Alexey,

 This still had some questions from me:

From my understanding ext4 commit is try to fix sync behavior that make sure we send a barrier 
to device to gurante cache is really flushed.

And Looking at jbd2 commit, it will issue barrier isn't it?

What commit transaction vs sync_fs make differences for ext4 is ext4_sync_fs
will try to flush any dirty data of this file, but commit transaction no gurantee
dirty data in order mode, the fix looks ok from first point of view.

But it doesn't look necessary from Lustre of point:
Because Lustre osd ldiskfs write dosen't dirty data and also
Any write will be protected by transaction:

1)start transaction
2)write data
3)end transaction..

So from Lustre point, if we call force_commit, Any write should be send to disk?
Because all write is protected by  transaction? It make no difference with ext4_sync_fs
here..

Anything I am missing here?

Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

patch makes sense for no journal quota case in that case we need call

dquot_writeback_dquots(sb, -1)

Anyway, ext4_sync_fs looks more safe than just force_commit in the future.
But this changelog is confusing a bit though

parents.subject: LU-8066 ofd: migrate from proc to sysfs

commit.subject: LU-11020 osd: use right sync
commit.message:
LU-11020 osd: use right sync

use a sync_fs instead of journal flush only.
ext4 commit with similar change

commit 06a407f13daf9e48f0ef7189c7e54082b53940c7
Author: Dmitry Monakhov <dmonakhov@openvz.org>
Date:   Wed Jun 12 22:25:07 2013 -0400

ext4: fix data integrity for ext4_sync_fs

Inode's data or non journaled quota may be written w/o jounral so we
 _must_ send a barrier at the end of ext4_sync_fs. But it can be
skipped if journal commit will do it for us.

Cray-bug-id: LUS-5993
Signed-off-by: Alexey Lyashkov <c17817@cray.com>
Change-Id: Ie242b48d0b8ad61cf4d38ac0fb173176193716d9
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/32866
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Wang Shilong <wshilong@ddn.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
